nodes.task: Fix issue #93 (3 duplicate tags + 2 <meta description> tags in the homepage server response Googlebot sees) and issue #101 (prerendered HTML hard-codes "0 trade-ups", "0 profitable", "0 data points" because the React counters fetch /api/global-stats client-side, which Googlebot doesn't see). ROOT CAUSE for #93: scripts/prerender.js runs Puppeteer over each public route and writes the rendered HTML back to dist/<route>/index.html. The captured HTML contains BOTH the original index.html <head> tags AND the React-Helmet-Async-injected duplicates. The homepage `/` is then served by `app.use(express.static(distPath))` (server/index.ts ~line 1012) without going through the existing dedupe logic in server/seo.ts:injectMetaIntoSpa, so the duplicates leak to crawlers. ROOT CAUSE for #101: the homepage's GlobalStatBar / hero counters render `<span>0</span>` initially and animate up after fetch("/api/global-stats"). The prerender script (scripts/prerender.js ~line 69) returns a stub `{ total_trade_ups: 0, ... }` for /api/global-stats during prerendering, so the captured HTML contains 0s. FIX: 1) Add an exported `dedupeHead(html: string): string` helper in `server/seo.ts`. It must keep ONLY the first occurrence of each of: `<title>...`, ``, ``, every `` tag (deduped by the X key), and every `` tag (deduped by the X key). Use simple string-scanning regexes that operate inside the `...` block. Empty `` tags must also be stripped (issue #93 shows one in the leaked HTML). Do NOT touch tags outside ``. 2) In `server/index.ts`, register a NEW `app.get("/", ...)` route handler BEFORE `app.use(express.static(distPath))` (around the current line 1012). The handler: - Reads `path.join(__dirname, "..", "dist", "index.html")` (same pattern as the other crawler handlers in this file). If dist/index.html does not exist (dev mode), call `next()`. - Calls `dedupeHead(html)` on the contents. - Queries the same data the existing `/api/global-stats` route returns. Read `server/routes/status.ts` to find the existing query — DO NOT re-implement; either import a helper from there (refactor to export it if needed) or duplicate the same SQL with a comment pointing at the source. - Replaces the three placeholder counter strings in the prerendered HTML with formatted real numbers using `Number.toLocaleString("en-US")`. The prerendered markers look like: `>0 trade-ups` `>0 profitable` `>0 data points` Match the literal `>0<` immediately preceding each label. Be defensive: if the prerendered HTML changes shape, fall back to leaving the placeholder untouched (don't throw). - Sets `Content-Type: text/html` and `Cache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate` (matches the existing `*` handler at line 1021). - On any DB error, logs and falls through to `next()` (so express.static still serves a stale-but-valid page). 3) Use TDD. Create `tests/unit/dedupeHead.test.ts` with cases: - 3 `` tags (one empty) → only the first non-empty title kept - 2 `<meta name="description">` → only first kept - duplicate `<link rel="canonical">` → only first kept - duplicate og:title → only first og:title kept; og:description (different key) untouched - duplicate twitter:image → only first kept - tags outside <head> untouched Run `npx vitest run tests/unit/dedupeHead.test.ts` — must fail before implementation, pass after. FILES (repo-relative): - modify: server/seo.ts (add dedupeHead export — do not change existing exports' signatures) - modify: server/index.ts (register `/` handler before express.static) - modify (optional): server/routes/status.ts — only if you choose to export a `getGlobalStats(pool)` helper for reuse - create: tests/unit/dedupeHead.test.ts GOTCHAS (from prior reviewer feedback — DO NOT repeat): - DO NOT modify AGENTS.md or package-lock.json. If your editor adds them to the diff, revert them. - Use `refs #93 #101` in your commit message, NOT `fixes #N` — Tim closes manually. - Pre-existing vitest baseline failures (DB schema mismatch, integration timeouts) are NOT your concern. Only verify `npx vitest run tests/unit/dedupeHead.test.ts` passes plus `npx tsc --noEmit` is clean. Skip running the full suite. - TypeScript: no `as any` or `as unknown as` casts. flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix issue #94 (36 pages return 5xx to Googlebot — stale trade-up IDs in sitemap-tradeups.xml) and issue #100 (86 pages "Crawled - currently not indexed" — empty sticker/agent/charm collection pages in sitemap-collections.xml).

FIX FOR #94 (Option A from the issue: drop the trade-ups sitemap entirely):
1) In `server/routes/sitemap.ts`:
   - Remove the `<sitemap><loc>${base}/sitemap-tradeups.xml</loc>...` line from `buildSitemapIndex`.
   - Delete the entire `router.get("/sitemap-tradeups.xml", ...)` handler (currently around lines 139–157).
2) In `server/daemon/index.ts` around lines 833–834, remove the block that writes `public/sitemap-tradeups.xml` from disk (the daemon-generated static sitemap; it is no longer served and should not be written). Keep all other daemon logic untouched.

FIX FOR #100 (filter empty collections):
1) In `server/routes/sitemap.ts`, modify the `/sitemap-collections.xml` route's SQL to only include collections that have at least one skin with a listing. Read `server/db.ts` first to confirm the actual column names — if the schema has `skins.collection_name` (text) and `listings.skin_id`, use:
     SELECT c.name FROM collections c
     WHERE EXISTS (
       SELECT 1 FROM skins s
       JOIN listings l ON l.skin_id = s.id
       WHERE s.collection_name = c.name
     )
     ORDER BY c.name
   If the actual schema is different (e.g. `skins.collection_id`), adapt the JOIN — but verify against the existing collections-related queries elsewhere in the codebase (grep for `FROM collections` in server/) before guessing.
2) Same treatment for `/sitemap-collection-tradeups.xml`: only include collections that have at least one active, non-theoretical, profitable trade-up. Use:
     SELECT DISTINCT ti.collection_name AS name
     FROM trade_up_inputs ti
     JOIN trade_ups t ON ti.trade_up_id = t.id
     WHERE t.listing_status = 'active'
       AND t.is_theoretical = false
       AND t.profit_cents > 0
     ORDER BY name

3) TDD. Create `tests/unit/sitemap.test.ts` with cases (no DB required — test pure builder fns):
   - `buildSitemapIndex(...)` does NOT contain the substring `sitemap-tradeups.xml`.
   - `buildCollectionSitemap(...)` correctly emits `<url>` entries from a passed-in collection list (it already accepts an array, so the empty-filter happens at the SQL layer — assert the builder still works for an empty list and a populated list).
   Run `npx vitest run tests/unit/sitemap.test.ts` — must fail first (because index still has the line), pass after.

FILES (repo-relative):
- modify: server/routes/sitemap.ts
- modify: server/daemon/index.ts (only the sitemap-tradeups.xml write block)
- create: tests/unit/sitemap.test.ts

GOTCHAS (from prior reviewer feedback):
- DO NOT modify AGENTS.md or package-lock.json.
- Use `refs #94 #100` in your commit message, NOT `fixes`.
- Verify only your test plus `npx tsc --noEmit`. Skip the full suite — pre-existing baseline failures are not your concern.
- TypeScript: no `as any` or `as unknown as`.
- Don't accidentally drop `sitemap-collection-tradeups.xml` (singular vs plural — the index correctly references both `sitemap-collection-tradeups.xml` AND used to reference `sitemap-tradeups.xml`. Only remove the latter.).

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix issue #102 (www.tradeupbot.app appearing in GSC as a separate property — link equity split). Add an Express-level 301 redirect from www → non-www so the redirect is consistent regardless of whether the nginx layer in front does it.

FIX:
1) In `server/index.ts`, add a redirect middleware at the TOP of the middleware chain (BEFORE `app.use(compression())` around line 83):
     app.use((req, res, next) => {
       const host = req.headers.host || "";
       if (host.toLowerCase() === "www.tradeupbot.app") {
         return res.redirect(301, `https://tradeupbot.app${req.originalUrl}`);
       }
       next();
     });
   The redirect must preserve the full path + querystring via `req.originalUrl`. Status MUST be 301 (permanent), not 302.
2) Verify all `<link rel="canonical">` URLs already use the non-www form. Grep `src/` and `server/` for `www.tradeupbot.app`. The only legitimate hits should be:
   - server/index.ts CORS allowlist (line ~87) — keep, www origin must still be permitted to make CORS preflights so the redirect can fire.
   - server/index.ts CSP `connectSrc` etc — keep (those are Google/Stripe www subdomains, unrelated).
   Any `canonical` / `og:url` / `twitter:url` references using `www.tradeupbot.app` must be changed to `tradeupbot.app`. Based on a quick grep there should be none today, but verify.
3) TDD. Create `tests/unit/host-redirect.test.ts` that tests the middleware in isolation by constructing fake req/res/next objects (no Express server needed). Cases:
   - host=`www.tradeupbot.app`, originalUrl=`/trade-ups?x=1` → res.redirect called with (301, `https://tradeupbot.app/trade-ups?x=1`); next() NOT called.
   - host=`WWW.TRADEUPBOT.APP` (mixed case) → still redirects (case-insensitive).
   - host=`tradeupbot.app` → next() called; res.redirect NOT called.
   - missing host header → next() called.
   Extract the middleware to a small named function inside server/index.ts (e.g. `function redirectWwwHost(req, res, next)`) and export it from a NEW small file `server/redirect-www.ts` so the test can import it without booting the whole app. Server/index.ts then imports and uses it.
   Run `npx vitest run tests/unit/host-redirect.test.ts` — must fail first, pass after.

FILES (repo-relative):
- create: server/redirect-www.ts (the named middleware)
- modify: server/index.ts (wire the middleware in at the top of the chain — be careful: this file is also touched by the homepage-seo node, so the merge must keep both edits intact)
- create: tests/unit/host-redirect.test.ts

GOTCHAS (from prior reviewer feedback):
- DO NOT modify AGENTS.md or package-lock.json.
- Use `refs #102` in your commit message, NOT `fixes`.
- Verify only your test plus `npx tsc --noEmit`. Skip the full suite.
- TypeScript: no `as any` or `as unknown as`. Type req/res/next using express types (`import type { Request, Response, NextFunction } from "express"`).
- Do not drop the `https://www.tradeupbot.app` entry from the CORS allowlist — preflight from www must still succeed for the redirect to be visible to browsers.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix issue #104 (single 195KB gzipped JS bundle for all routes — no route-based code splitting). The repo already lazy-loads a few heavy components (DataViewer, CollectionViewer, CalculatorPage, SkinPage, TradeUpSharePage — see existing `lazy(() => import(...))` calls in src/App.tsx lines 22-27). Extend that pattern to the remaining page modules so each route ships its own chunk. FIX: 1) In `src/App.tsx`, convert these eager imports (currently at the top of the file) to React.lazy: LandingPage, TradeUpsPage, FaqPage, TermsPage, PrivacyPage, FeaturesPage, PricingPage, MyTradeUpsPage, ListingSniperPage, BlogPage, BlogPostPage Use the same shape as the existing lazy imports. Note that some of these are NAMED exports (e.g. `import { LandingPage } from './pages/LandingPage.js'`) and `lazy()` requires a default — wrap with `.then(m => ({ default: m.LandingPage }))` for named, OR use the existing default export if present (read each file before deciding). Check each pages/* file's export shape first. 2) Wrap the `` block in src/App.tsx with a top-level ``. Reuse the existing fallback pattern already used inline in `CollectionPage` (line ~89): `
Loading
`. 3) Verify with `npm run build` that the build succeeds AND `dist/assets/` contains MULTIPLE JS chunks (not a single index-*.js). Eyeball the chunk sizes — none should exceed the original ~200KB. The main chunk should drop noticeably. 4) Run `npx tsc --noEmit` — must be clean. 5) Run any existing tests that import App.tsx (grep for `from "../App"` or similar in tests/) — they must still pass. If no App.tsx tests exist, no new test is required for this node — the build itself is the verification. FILES (repo-relative): - modify: src/App.tsx GOTCHAS (from prior reviewer feedback): - DO NOT modify AGENTS.md or package-lock.json. - Use `refs #104` in the commit message. - Pre-existing vitest baseline failures are not your concern. The build (`npm run build`) and `npx tsc --noEmit` ARE your concern. - Don't introduce a Suspense fallback inside every route — one wrapping the whole `` is enough. Nested Suspense (inside CollectionPage etc) can stay. - Watch for `useNavigate`, `useParams`, `useLocation` etc. — those are NOT lazy and must remain as eager imports from react-router-dom. - Do NOT change the structure of route definitions — just swap eager components for lazy ones. flt workflow pass when build is clean, tsc is clean, and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix issue #98 (1.5MB of unoptimized marketing PNGs on the landing page — collections.png 559KB, expanded.png 411KB, dataviewer.png 345KB, tradeuptable.png 228KB). Convert each to a JPEG <500KB and update all references. FIX: 1) Convert each PNG in `public/` to JPEG using macOS `sips` (built into darwin — no install needed). Start at quality 80; if any output exceeds 500KB, drop to 65 and retry. sips -s format jpeg --setProperty formatOptions 80 public/collections.png --out public/collections.jpg sips -s format jpeg --setProperty formatOptions 80 public/expanded.png --out public/expanded.jpg sips -s format jpeg --setProperty formatOptions 80 public/dataviewer.png --out public/dataviewer.jpg sips -s format jpeg --setProperty formatOptions 80 public/tradeuptable.png --out public/tradeuptable.jpg After confirming all four JPEGs exist and each is < 500KB (use `ls -la public/*.jpg`), DELETE the original PNGs: rm public/collections.png public/expanded.png public/dataviewer.png public/tradeuptable.png Do NOT touch `public/dreams&nightmares-collection.png` — that is a separate asset, leave it alone. 2) Update all references. Grep `src/`, `server/`, `index.html`, and `content/` for each of the four filenames and change `.png` → `.jpg`. Likely sites of references: - `src/pages/LandingPage.tsx` (the marketing `` tags — already touched in commit 0748685; preserve any existing `loading="lazy"`, `width`, `height` attrs and only swap the src extension) - `index.html` (`og:image`, `twitter:image` meta tags — line 12 and 18 reference `tradeuptable.png`) - `server/seo.ts` (the default `ogImage = "https://tradeupbot.app/tradeuptable.png"` constants — there are TWO occurrences, line ~25 and line ~82) - any blog post markdown in `content/` if relevant - any sitemap or SSR HTML in `server/index.ts` that references these filenames 3) TDD. Create `tests/unit/marketing-assets.test.ts` that uses Node `fs.statSync` to assert: - `public/collections.jpg` exists AND is < 500_000 bytes - same for expanded.jpg, dataviewer.jpg, tradeuptable.jpg - `public/collections.png` does NOT exist (and same for the other three originals) Run `npx vitest run tests/unit/marketing-assets.test.ts` — must fail before conversion, pass after. 4) `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. `npm run build` should still succeed. FILES (repo-relative): - create: public/collections.jpg, public/expanded.jpg, public/dataviewer.jpg, public/tradeuptable.jpg - delete: public/collections.png, public/expanded.png, public/dataviewer.png, public/tradeuptable.png - modify: src/pages/LandingPage.tsx (image src refs) - modify: index.html (og:image, twitter:image) - modify: server/seo.ts (default ogImage constants — note this file is also touched by homepage-seo node; the depends_on on homepage-seo ensures sequencing) - modify: any other file found by grep that references the four PNG filenames - create: tests/unit/marketing-assets.test.ts GOTCHAS (from prior reviewer feedback): - DO NOT modify AGENTS.md or package-lock.json. - Use `refs #98` in commit message. - Verify only your test plus `npx tsc --noEmit` plus `npm run build`. Skip the full suite. - macOS `sips` is required and is a built-in on darwin — do NOT npm-install sharp/imagemin/cwebp; that would pollute package.json. - `git add` the new .jpg files explicitly and `git rm` the old .png files. Do not let the diff accidentally re-add the PNGs. - Don't touch the OG image dimensions — both .png and .jpg can coexist with the same Twitter Card type 'summary_large_image'. flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.
